title: Cursor 0.5 Release Steps Up In Vibe Coding
author: Tom Smykowski
text:
Member-only story
Cursor 0.5 Release Steps Up In Vibe Coding
4 min readMay 20, 2025
Cursor isn’t anywhere behind Copilot and Windsurf, it’s ahead of the game, so if you didn’t try it out, update to 0.5 and try to vibe code with Cursor
Cursor is a popular fork of VSCode that introduces its own set of AI features — separate from GitHub Copilot — designed for developers exploring AI-assisted coding, or those who want to vibe code all day long.
The release launched on May 10, 2025, brings some impressive features that put Cursor in direct competition with Copilot and Windsurf.
Simplified Pricing
First off, the pricing model has been simplified — it’s now request-based.
Here’s a quick overview of the current pricing:
- Free plan: 2,000 completions and 50 slow requests per month.
- Pro plan ($20/month): Unlimited completions, 500 requests per month, and unlimited slow requests.
- Enterprise plan ($40/user/month): Adds organization-wide privacy controls, centralized billing, and more.
Premium models include GPT-4, GPT-4o, Claude 3.5, and the latest Claude 3.7 Sonnet.
